coverage_note: The bundled SH3PXD2B population database is literature oriented and does not include a complete allele-frequency panel. Interpretation should prioritize current ClinVar/gnomAD status, zygosity, phase, segregation, copy-number breakpoint, transcript effect, phenotype match, and the definitive autosomal-recessive ClinGen curation.
gene_population_patterns_intro: Broader population patterns curated from SH3PXD2B/TKS4 rare-disease, podosome, extracellular-matrix, collagen-remodeling, expression, and cancer-cell-migration literature.

sources.label: NCBI Gene 285590: SH3PXD2B expression, GO context, bibliography, and phenotype links
Human Protein Atlas: SH3PXD2B low tissue specificity, cytoplasmic expression, and plasma-membrane/nucleoplasm/cytosol localization
ClinGen Gene Facts: definitive AR Frank-ter Haar syndrome gene-disease validity
PMCID PMC2820172: SH3PXD2B FTHS mutation spectrum and developmental model

gene_population_patterns.summary: NCBI and Human Protein Atlas resources frame SH3PXD2B as broadly expressed or low tissue specificity, with cytoplasmic expression in many cell types including fibroblasts and plasma-membrane/nucleoplasm/cytosol localization context.

ClinGen curates SH3PXD2B-Frank-ter Haar syndrome as definitive with autosomal-recessive mode of inheritance; pathogenic interpretation therefore depends strongly on biallelic pathogenic dosage and phenotype fit.
This splice-acceptor variant is a pathogenic FTHS marker in ClinVar/OMIM literature, with GRCh37 coordinate chr5:171849502 and transcript-level c.76-2A>C notation.

Reported homozygous SH3PXD2B mutations, deletions, and exon 13 deletion support a TKS4 loss mechanism affecting podosome function, ECM degradation, collagen remodeling, skeletal/ocular/cardiac features, dermal fibrosis, and contractures.

TKS4 participates in podosome/invadopodia formation and ECM degradation, so cancer and epithelial-plasticity literature is useful for pathway context but should not be treated as a germline cancer-risk prediction.

Bundled EPIC probes provide local promoter and gene-body regulatory context, but no population methylation threshold is bundled for FTHS, BDCS, collagen remodeling, ocular disease, fibrosis, or cancer migration.
